description_long: This is a rustic wooden fence constructed from roughly cylindrical logs, featuring four horizontal rails attached to vertical posts. The wood appears untreated or minimally finished, giving it a natural, weathered look with visible grain and slight irregularities in the rails. The fence is composed of two main sections joined at a central post, with the rails unevenly spaced and slightly curved, contributing to its handmade appearance. There are no decorative elements or embellishments, and the ends of the posts are flat without any caps. The structure is open, allowing visibility and airflow, and is typical of rural or park settings but could also be found in urban green spaces. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function over ornamentation.
description: A rustic, natural wood fence with four uneven horizontal rails and flat-topped posts, joined at the center. The design is simple and functional, with a weathered, handmade appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the fence, with four horizontal rails and two main sections joined at a central post.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the rails and posts.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the same four rails and central joining post.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the arrangement of the rails and posts.
